title: Settings

language.hint: Auto follows your OS language; other options force a specific one. Saved instantly — no need to press Save below. The same switcher (compact, flag-only) lives in the top-right header.

output.hint: Use Documents sets your OS "Documents" folder and saves settings. You can change it anytime with Browse.

mode_summary.local: No API key. You need the whisper-cli executable and one ggml .bin model on disk.
mode_summary.browser: No API key or whisper-cli. Transcription runs in the app (WASM); pick model size — first run may download weights.
mode_summary.cloud: Uses your provider's API key. whisper-cli and local models are not used.

transcription.browser_warn: Experimental: needs internet for the first model download; long audio may be slow or run out of memory. If this fails, switch to Cloud API or Local Whisper.

local_whisper.step1_hint: Windows: official whisper-bin-x64.zip from ggml-org/whisper.cpp (MIT) — use the button below to download into app data (includes DLLs). macOS: no CLI zip in those releases; the button runs which whisper-cli / whisper / main and scans Homebrew paths, or use Pick file… after brew install whisper-cpp.
local_whisper.linux_intro: Linux: this app does not download whisper-cli here. Install a package or build from source, then set the path below or use Pick file….
local_whisper.linux_li_ubuntu: Ubuntu / Debian: sudo apt install whisper-cpp (if available in your release) or build from ggml-org/whisper.cpp
local_whisper.linux_li_fedora: Fedora: sudo dnf install whisper-cpp (package name may vary)
local_whisper.path_field_label: Path to executable (optional if next to app)
local_whisper.path_placeholder: whisper-cli.exe / whisper-cli — or use Pick file…
local_whisper.install_success: whisper-cli path saved. The checklist should update after refresh.
local_whisper.step15_hint: Picks which whisper.cpp build to download. CUDA needs an NVIDIA GPU (10-20× faster on RTX-class hardware). Vulkan works on most NVIDIA / AMD / Intel GPUs (8-15×). CPU is the safe baseline. Changing this does not auto-redownload; click Re-download whisper-cli below to fetch the matching bundle.
local_whisper.gpu_auto_other: — Auto stays on CPU; pick Vulkan manually if drivers are current.
local_whisper.step2_hint: Choose a model size, then download. The checklist turns green when the file exists and the SHA-1 matches the catalog (same check as after download).

browser_whisper.model_size_hint: Same labels as the local catalog; the app maps them to Transformers.js checkpoints. No ggml .bin download here — weights load on first transcription (needs network once per model).

cloud.where_key_summary: Where do I get an API key?
cloud.where_key_body_provider: Use a provider with OpenAI-style POST …/audio/transcriptions and JSON with a text field.
cloud.where_key_warning: Treat the key like a password: do not share it or paste it into public chats or git.
cloud.credential_store_hint: API key is saved in the OS credential store (Windows Credential Manager, macOS Keychain, Secret Service on Linux). The settings file keeps other options only (no key in plain text).

media_tools.win_hint: Download for me (Windows): fetches yt-dlp.exe from GitHub releases and a GPL FFmpeg zip from BtbN/FFmpeg-Builds, extracts ffmpeg.exe into your app data folder, then saves paths in settings.
media_tools.mac_hint: Download for me (macOS): fetches yt-dlp_macos from GitHub releases and a static ffmpeg for your CPU (ffmpeg-static), saves both under app data and updates paths. If macOS blocks them, allow in Privacy & Security or clear quarantine (xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine …).
media_tools.linux_hint: One-click download is only on Windows and macOS. On Linux install ffmpeg and yt-dlp (e.g. package manager) and paste full paths below.
media_tools.self_install_intro: Put ffmpeg and yt-dlp next to v2t.exe (or in a bin subfolder), or enter full paths here.
media_tools.js_runtimes_hint: If YouTube fails with "no supported JavaScript runtime", install Deno or Node and set this to the runtime name yt-dlp expects (often deno).
media_tools.deno_success: Done. Deno installed; JS runtimes set to deno.
media_tools.cookies_label: Cookies source for yt-dlp (YouTube / TikTok age-gate)

cookies_options.auto: Auto (Edge on Windows, Chrome on macOS, Firefox on Linux)
cookies_options.none: Disabled — do not use browser cookies

media_tools.cookies_hint: Passes --cookies-from-browser to yt-dlp. The browser must be installed and you must be logged in to YouTube / TikTok in it. Chrome, Brave and Edge have two known issues on Windows: (1) their cookie database is locked while the browser is running — close it first; (2) since Chrome 127+ cookies are encrypted with app-bound encryption (DPAPI) that yt-dlp cannot decrypt even when the browser is closed (issue #10927). Firefox is the most reliable option — log in there and select Firefox.
media_tools.tools_msg_done: ffmpeg and yt-dlp saved to app data and paths updated.

keep_video: Save downloaded video to output folder (URL jobs — second yt-dlp pass, best mp4)
keep_audio: Save downloaded audio to output folder (URL jobs + local video — extracted via ffmpeg)

subtitles.intro: For YouTube videos with manual subtitles in a priority language, fetch the SRT directly via yt-dlp and skip download + Whisper. Auto-generated captions are intentionally ignored (lower quality than Whisper-medium for non-English). Single-video URLs only — pure-playlist URLs continue to download + transcribe normally.
subtitles.priority_hint: First match wins. Regional variants are matched (e.g. en matches en-US).
subtitles.keep_srt: Also save the original .srt next to the .txt transcript
